Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon; and

Require the MSWDO to exert effort to ensure that all IATF copy of the Social Amelioration Cards shall be duly filled out.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Other recommendations were already fully implemented except for the following:

Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/ understatement of the RPT/SET Receivables and their related accounts taking into considerations the corrections on the errors in the Summary of Collections and the advance payments of provincial and barangay shares already remitted; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the correct accounting entries in recording RPT/SET collections; and
observations.managementAction:
Accounting Office is currently coordinating with the Treasury Office for the proper flow of RPT/SEF collections.




Accounting Office is on the process of understanding the RPT/SEF transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the management to provide justification that the honoraria or allowance received by the MBOC is not similar to the honorarium or allowance being given by the COMELEC, otherwise it shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 | No concrete action taken by the LGU

observations.recommendation:
Other recommendations were already fully implemented except for the following:

Require the LDRRMO and Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the proper National Government Agency for the infrastructure audit of the evacuation centers or selection of the suitable sites and proper design in case of building permanent evacuation centers, as the case may be. Require the LDRRMO to take concrete actions on herein noted lacking facilities and the deficiencies noted in infrastructure audit, if any.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Instructed the concerned personnel to coordinate with the proper NGA for infra audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which should be the basis of the accounting to set-up RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2019, 2020 and 2021 and for the years thereafter, for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately apply the correct accounting entries in recording RPT/SET collections in order to avoid subsequent consequential erroneous entries; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to review all journal entries of prior periods to correct the erroneous accounting entries made in recording RPT/SET collections to attain the same balances for RPT/SET Receivable and its corresponding Deferred Tax Income accounts.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the Supply Officer to submit Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) to COA within 24 hours from acceptance of the delivered supplies and materials; and
Enjoin the General Service Unit to prepare and maintain a logbook to ensure that Purchase Orders (PO) and Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) are numbered chronologically to avoid repetition of PO/IAR numbers.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documents for inspection and to ensure that on future request for inspection, a complete set of documents will be submitted based on checklist enumerated in Annex “C” of COA Circular 2009.

We recommended to the management to:
Ensure that registration of all serviceable motor vehicles owned by the LGU be renewed in CY 2022 and for the years thereafter; and
Require the GSO to have an updated monitoring of all motor vehicles with information on the Certificate of Registration (CR) and Official Receipt (OR) to facilitate the timely renewal of registration with the LTO to avoid penalties for late payment and payment of damages for third party liabilities in case of accidents. Also, ensure that the GSO shall file a copy of the OR/CR.

We recommended and management agreed to establish written guidelines on the receipt, distribution and reporting of donations in-kind received by the LGU to enhance transparency and accountability over the donations taking into consideration the centralization on the accounting of donated goods by vesting upon a single office or body the sole authority and responsibility relative to in-kind donations.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Direct the MDRRMO to submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in its prescribed format duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month; and
Require the MDRRM Council to incorporate a separate caption in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan, the programs/projects/activities chargeable against the unexpended balances of prior years to maximize its use for speedy realization of the LDRRM objectives.

We recommended to the management to procure enough relief goods such as food and non-food items as a stockpile for immediate response in case of occurrence of unforeseen disaster and establish a reorder point to ensure sufficient stockpile of goods ready for relief operation in case of calamities.

We have recommended the following:

Refrain from granting of additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made; 

Demand immediate settlement or liquidation of cash advances and/or cause the withholding of the amount due from the concerned officials and employees to compel them to settle their accounts. Management may resort to legal action if no settlement has been made after sending three (3) demand letters; and

File a request for authority to write-off for the accounts that are deemed dormant and liquidation is futile, to be guided by the procedures contained in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2020, 2019 & 2018 | Applied only on cash advances for payroll.


Send demand letters.

AAR 2020 | Adjusted the books of the LGU by debiting the ‘Due from LGUs’ account and crediting the ‘Due to LGUs’ account.


Barangays already liquidate the fund. 


Shall ensure that future fund transfers to barangays are covered with MOA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Charge the above expenditure against the General Fund of the Municipality and reimburse the BGCM account with the gross amount of ₱293,725.00; and


Adhere strictly with the provisions and guidelines set forth under LBC 125 in identifying the programs, projects and activities funded under the BGCM fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Reimbursed the BGCM from the General Fund

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Require the Accounting Office to debit the Inventory account upon purchases and credit the same upon distribution or receipt of RSMI from the GSO/end users;

Direct the Supply Officer to issue Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon issuance of supplies to end-user and summarize monthly the issued RIS in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis in recognizing the expense accounts; and

Require the periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. Physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2020 | Debited Inventory account upon purchase.


Issued RIS however, SSMI were not prepared.




Conducted physical count on unissued inventory items.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Enjoin the Supply Officer to strictly implement control over the usage of motor vehicle by requiring properly accomplished, duly approved and serially numbered driver’s trip tickets before issuing Order Slips;

Enjoin the Supply Officer to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per motor Vehicle and submit to Local COA for verification purposes; and

Enjoin the designated drivers to secure Charge or Sales Invoice from the gasoline station and submit the same to the Supply Officer for check and balance purposes.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2020 | Payment of gasoline were attached with driver’s trip tickets.


Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption were prepared.


Charge/Sales Invoice were attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to ensure that Disbursement Vouchers for the cash advance for payroll is supported with Certification from the Municipal Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books, Approved Payroll, and Daily Time Records (DTR) approved by the supervisor and ensure that only those personnel who had submitted their DTRs shall be included in the payroll to have accurate computation of the net pay.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Disbursement Vouchers for the cash advance for payroll were properly attached with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to limit the grant of cash advance to SDO for petty AICS expenditure sufficient to the requirement of one month and shall replenish when the disbursement reach at least 75%, or five (5) days after the end of each month or as the need requires by submitting a replenishment voucher with all the supporting documents duly summarized in a report of disbursement. Also, the management shall establish process flow on the payment of petty AICS having it pass through internal audit to ensure propriety of such payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The management had set a limit on the amount of cash advance to be granted.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to maintain property ledgers and property cards respectively; and


Require the Supply Officer to conduct actual and regular physical count of all assets annually and undertake the necessary reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the balance per books.

Demanded immediate settlement of cash advances



Limits the granting of CA to NGA officials


Prohibit the granting of cash advances to employees that are not duly designated as disbursing officer and are not bonded.


No action taken.

Could not be implemented due to lack of turn-over from previous MAO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which should be the basis of the accounting to set-up RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year to include CY 2019; and
Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the completion of the general revision of assessments to determine the accurate real property taxes due.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT/SET Receivables and their related accounts taking into considerations the corrections on the errors in the Summary of Collections and the advance payments of provincial and barangay shares already remitted;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the correct accounting entries in recording RPT/SET collections; and
Request from the Municipal Treasurer to enhance the preparation of the Summary of Collections to eliminate errors and if error occurs, it can be easily detected at their level.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the refund of ₱84,000 by the officials and employees who authorized the grant and received the Productivity Incentive Bonus for CY 2018 and ensure that all future bonuses and allowances granted to personnel are duly supported with legal basis to prevent disallowance; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the payrolls and DV of the said claims.

We recommended to the management to provide legal basis citing that the said payment of honoraria to the MBOC shall be sourced from the LGU fund and submit a certificate of non-payment of honoraria from the COMELEC Central Office Accountant. Otherwise, request reimbursement from the COMELEC for the total election expenses incurred during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections in accordance with Section 10 of the Omnibus Election Code.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Submit immediately the assignment or detail orders coming from the Chief of the Philippine National Police enjoining the PNP personnel to act as protective security to the Local Chief Executive; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

We recommended to the management to:
Enjoin all Job Order workers to attach their actual accomplishment reports and DTRs duly reviewed and verified by their respective immediate supervisors; and
Enjoin the Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to Job Order workers are supported with accomplishment reports for actual services rendered aside from duly approved DTRs.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere to the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 in conducting future similar activities to be within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality and limit the number of participants to only those indispensable for the objective of the activity in conformity to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.
We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than for official traveling expenses of their own.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the Department Heads to include fuel, oil and lubricants in the preparation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) that is to be reflected in the LGU’s Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order for the BAC to process the procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants in adherence to R.A. 9184 and discontinue the practice of reimbursement.

We recommended to the management to direct its Human Resource Management Office to:
Deduct the forfeited unused mandatory/forced leave;
Review and update all leave cards and ensure correctness of leave balances; and
Record the earned leave credits on a monthly basis.

We recommended to the management to:
Instruct their canvassers to require the prospective suppliers to indicate the brand names and other detailed item specifications;
Enjoin the BAC to conduct evaluation as to the most responsive bid based on brand and other qualitative aspect of the goods; and
Direct the General Services Unit to prepare all the purchase orders entered into by the LGU and accomplish properly the P.O.s including the brand names and other detailed specifications of goods to be procured in order to serve the purpose for which the purchase order was prescribed.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the Municipal Engineer, the BAC and other concerned personnel the immediate submission of the documents to avoid further delay in the conduct of technical review and inspection of the LGU’s projects. Moreover, ensure that documents submitted for contract review shall be complete.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors to post Warranty Security before final payment as prescribed in Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for similar transactions in the future.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with her predecessor of any concern regarding BRS;
Require the Municipal Accountant to secure monthly snap shot of all the bank accounts of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of BRS; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly BRS and to submit to the audit team all BRS due for submission and henceforth observe the prescribed timeline.

We recommended to the management to:
Establish a more efficient collection procedure in order to minimize the workloads and the errors committed as well as to save resources of the LGU; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to review all the transactions and Journal Entry Vouchers pertaining to the affected accounts and adjust erroneous entries committed.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to record all fund releases to DCSPGs and liquidations using the following entries:
Upon release to DCSPG:
Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations     xxx
Cash in Bank, LCCA					      xxx
Upon liquidation:
Due to NGAs					         xxx
Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations	  xxx

We recommended that the management should require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate return of the PDAF in the amount of ₱500,000 to the national government thru the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the LGU counterpart to Trust Fund upon receipt of fund from the National Government;
Require the MPDO and the Municipal Engineer to consider the total fund, including the LGU counterpart, in the preparation of Program of Works (POW); and
Utilize the fund strictly to the specific purpose to which it has been intended.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Stop the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than for official traveling expenses of their own; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer-ICO to request assistance from uniformed personnel in drawing significant amount of cash advance to safeguard government funds from loss caused by robbery.

We recommended to management to:
Enjoin all job order workers to attach their actual accomplishment reports to their DTRs to be reviewed and verified by their respective immediate supervisors; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to job order workers are supported with their accomplishment reports for actual services rendered aside from duly approved DTRs.

We recommended to the management to:
Maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development;
Enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all officials responsible in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to strictly observe the guidelines set forth under DILG – DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in identifying the programs, projects and activities to funded under the 20% Development Fund; and
Realign the unobligated balance of non-developmental projects totaling ₱200,000 to projects and programs in accordance with the guidelines set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management committed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval.

We recommended that management instruct the municipal accountant to prepare a Reconciliation of the General and Subsidiary Ledgers, substantiated with corroborating documents issued by COA if settlements were made thereon, and instruct the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to exert diligent efforts for the production of demand letters and collection of the disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Instruct the MENRO to intensify waste segregation campaign enforcing penalties for violations as set forth in the Municipal Solid Waste Management Code.
Direct the MENRO to maintain adequate inventory of protective gears/supplies for use by the garbage collectors and segregators.
Ensure that minimum considerations for controlled dumpsite be met tapping DENR and PENRO to ensure compliance.
Integrate provision for monitoring on solid waste management practices of the other 9 barangays in the municipality.
observations.reason: The municipality had conducted waste segregation campaign to its component barangays and requires the latter to establish material recovery facility. Also, dumpsite of the municipality has been properly fenced as minimum requirement of the law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure compliance with Sec. 4 of AO 17 dated July 28, 2011 in the purchase of common-use supplies, and require the attachment of Certificate of Non-Availability if items procured are not available in the DBM- Procurement Service.
observations.reason: Procurement from outside suppliers was only resorted to when not available in the  DBM- Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and MEEDO Officer to maintain subsidiary ledgers and special account for economic enterprises and require financial reporting so that its operations and accounts are monitored.
observations.reason: The municipality was able to craft business plan on its MEEDO and a subsidiary has been maintained by the accountant.
